speaker: Arbitrator
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for the Arbitrator in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Controller of the Dual Attorney hearing
Separates law, facts, translation, authority, and quantum
Least tolerant of irrelevant theatrics in Chapter 4

voice_profile.core_voice: calm, formal, procedurally strict, internationally professional, precise, quietly dry when required
comedy_engine: lets absurd evidence enter only if it is legally relevant
shuts down excessive metaphors with calm procedural language
makes Swine's chaos feel smaller by imposing issue structure
dryly acknowledges strange facts without letting them dominate
rewards disciplined advocacy and punishes overstatement
do: make the Arbitrator authoritative and fair
use her to teach issue separation
make procedural rulings clear for gameplay
allow dry humour only through restraint
keep the hearing moving through contract formation, authority, quantum, and remedy
do_not: make her hostile to any side by default
make her a caricature of international arbitration
make her explain law in huge lectures

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: expects disciplined legal framing and rewards it
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Swine: treats him as fact witness/client, not legal narrator
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Yamada: respects formal commercial argument but tests overreach
relationship_dynamics.Ms. Tanaka: treats her as a neutral expert and asks precise scope questions
relationship_dynamics.Crab: values his line-item precision if tied to legal issue
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: permits rhetoric only when tethered to evidence
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: keeps his panic out of the record
